title: The KdV conservative flux of a constant state has zero flux difference

source.notes: Links the KdV flux scaffold to the generic fluxDifference operator.

informal.statement: For a constant grid state, the KdV-like conservative flux is constant, so its local flux difference is zero.
informal.latex: If \(u_i=c\), then \(F_{i+1}-F_i=0\) for the KdV-like flux.
assumptions: nonlinear, dispersion, and c are real numbers
i is a natural-number grid index
informal.expected_conclusion: The local flux difference of the constant-state KdV flux is zero.

declarations.name: fluxDifference
declarations.statement: def fluxDifference (flux : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := flux (i + 1) - flux i

declarations.name: kdvConservativeFlux_const_difference_zero
declarations.statement: theorem kdvConservativeFlux_const_difference_zero (nonlinear dispersion c : Real) (i : Nat) : fluxDifference (kdvConservativeFlux nonlinear dispersion (fun _ => c)) i = 0

tasks.input: Translate the zero flux-difference property for the KdV flux of a constant state into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem kdvConservativeFlux_const_difference_zero (nonlinear dispersion c : Real) (i : Nat) : fluxDifference (kdvConservativeFlux nonlinear dispersion (fun _ => c)) i = 0

tasks.input: Given fluxDifference and kdvConservativeFlux, complete the proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding definitions and closing with ring.
